channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Why Islam Seeks Shelter Under The Banner Of The Left
item.description: Islam is not a decorative addition to the progressive cause; it is on the verge of becoming its animating spirit.

item.title: Kristi Noem Rides off into the Sunset
item.description: The ousted DHS secretary took a serious role and made it like something out of Veep .

item.title: US signals readiness to escort tankers through Hormuz as traffic thins but no mission launched
item.description: Trump and Energy Secretary Chris Wright signal the U.S. may escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz as shipping traffic drops, though a U.S. offic...

item.title: White House shrugs off report of Russia helping Iran to target U.S. forces in Middle East
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt brushed off reports that Russia is providing Iran with intelligence to attack American forces in the Midd...

item.title: Hamit Coskunâs Victories and Losses
item.description: The man who burned a Koran in England won the most recent appeal. But the decision is not a win for free expression.

item.title: Kristi Noem's firing fails to sway Democrats as DHS shutdown drags on
item.description: Democrats maintained their DHS funding blockade over ICE reforms despite Secretary Kristi Noem's ouster, and Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., will take...

item.title: Lawmakers urging FISA reforms likely to face familiar pushback
item.description: A bipartisan group of lawmakers hopes to persuade the White House to support legislation that provides greater transparency and safeguards against pri...

item.title: Obama, Biden, Clintons remember Jesse Jackson in Chicago memorial service
item.description: Rev. Jesse Jackson's memorial service draws former Presidents Barack Obama, Joe Biden and Bill Clinton to Chicago's House of Hope arena on Friday.

item.title: Nearly 1,000 workers laid off at SK Battery plant in Georgia as companies cancel EVs and Trump Admin eliminates auto company incentives
item.description: Ford announced it was cancelling the all-electric Ford F-150 Lightning in December. SK Battery America Inc., a supplier of the Ford truck, laid off ne...

item.title: Blue State Failure Is Destroying Federalism And The Constitution
item.description: Washington, D.C., is now in charge of making sure that dogs in Los Angeles aren't mistreated.

item.title: Appeals Court Rules D.C.âs Gun Magazine Restriction Is Unconstitutional
item.description: 'Because these magazines are arms in common and ubiquitous use by law abiding citizens across this country, we agree ... that the Districtâs outright ...

item.title: Marine veteran vows to stay in North Carolina's US Senate race after violent Capitol arrest
item.description: Marine veteran and Green Party candidate Brian McGinnis remains defiant after a violent removal from a Senate hearing involving Sen. Tim Sheehy.

item.title: A Minneapolis Fed report details how much Trumpâs immigration crackdown hurt businesses and workers. âThere are not any people to hireâ
item.description: "We are hiring now to replace these workers or get more reliable alternatives, but there are not any people to hire."

item.title: Marjorie Taylor Greene says Tucker Carlson could beat Trump in an election
item.description: Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene is throwing her support behind Tucker Carlson for president after President Trump said the conservative commentator...

item.title: FLASHBACK: Dem Senate nominee called illegal aliens 'constituents,' gave advice on evading ICE
item.description: Texas Senate candidate James Talarico faces scrutiny over 2019 posts advising undocumented immigrants how to evade ICE detention during Trump's first ...

item.title: Trump administration unveils $20B reinsurance program to help maritime traffic in Middle East
item.description: The Trump administration is rolling out a $20 billion reinsurance program for ships that are worried about incurring losses due to the U.S.-Israeli on...

item.title: Applying Iraqâs Hardest Lessons to Iran
item.description: While Iran is not Iraq 2.0, it requires a disciplined approach and a clear plan for the future.

item.title: The Paramount Purchase Could Revive American Excellence In Hollywood
item.description: Paramount CEO David Ellison understands that pro-American films and television are worth investing in.

item.title: The Week: Trump Goes to War
item.description: Plus: Goodbye, Kristi Noem.

item.title: OpenAI investor Vinod Khosla believes AI will be able to do 80% of all jobs by 2030. Hereâs how life could be affordable after mass unemployment

item.title: New York AG Letitia James says Border Patrol account of migrant death in Buffalo 'unreliable'
item.description: New York Attorney General Letitia James questioned the Border Patrol's version of events that led up to the death of a migrant in the state late last ...

item.title: February was the biggest month in venture history, thanks only to OpenAI, Anthropic, and Waymo
item.description: Startup funding surged to a historic $189 billion in February, driven almost entirely by three AI mega-rounds that captured 83% of the total.

item.title: Noem Out
item.description: The former South Dakota governor was never well suited to the job.

item.title: Silicon Valley investor Vinod Khosla predicts education will be free, and the future of college âis a real questionâ
item.description: The billionaire VC says AI raises questions about how we value expertise.
